title: What does Unit 00s face look like
display_title: What does Unit 00s face look like
wikitext:
[]: What does Unit 00's face look like?             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Sun Mar 24, 2013 1:16 pm Something occurred to me: we never actually see Unit 00's face. We see Unit 01's ugly mug several times, and in End of Evangelion we see Unit 02's brutalized noggin, but Unit 00 has never had its bare head revealed. Now, if I remember rightly, design schematics on a screen in an episode show that behind the eye-lense are 5 smaller lenses. However, this doesn't really clear things up, as they are still part of the helmet. Also, whilst we do see the skulls of the aborted Unit 00 prototypes, the skulls are all very different, from almost human to hideously deformed. The only true representation of Unit 00's bonce is the Unit 00 Soul of Chogokin SPEC figure, which has removable armour, and includes a bare head. However, I have never actually seen said bear head. Does anyone have pictures of the bare head of the figure, and are there any actual images from the series/films of Zerogoki's unarmoured mush? Thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Mar 24, 2013 1:57 pm Reichu knows for sure. Check her blog where she makes sculptures of the Evas, if you dare. . . It's essentially a one-eyed Eva Unit-01.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Mar 24, 2013 3:18 pm What about the "eye" on top of its head? [wkimg:3dldug7g]05_C012a.jpg[/wkimg:3dldug7g] [wkimg:3dldug7g]05_C012b.jpg[/wkimg:3dldug7g] [wkimg:3dldug7g]05_C012c.jpg[/wkimg:3dldug7g]

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Sun Mar 24, 2013 3:40 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: What about the "eye" on top of its head? [wkimg]05_C012a.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]05_C012b.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]05_C012c.jpg[/wkimg]

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sun Mar 24, 2013 4:31 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: What about the "eye" on top of its head? [wkimg]05_C012a.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]05_C012b.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]05_C012c.jpg[/wkimg]

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Mar 24, 2013 5:19 pm Mr. Tines? Where are you when I need you? No, Reichu portrays Zerogouki as a cyclops.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Mar 24, 2013 5:23 pm Stryker wrote: Mr. Tines? Where are you when I need you?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Mar 24, 2013 6:37 pm Squigsquasher wrote: Correct me if I'm wrong, but I think that that's supposed to be a sensor dome rather than an eye.

[]:             Quote Post by hwdge » Thu Mar 28, 2013 6:34 pm probably something extremely deep and ambiguous/meaningful/ambiguous which will eventually escalate the discussion of it into a full blown civil war with 3 sides all arguing over, 1 eye, 2 eyes in normal position, and LOLOL CYCOPS WITH SCALP EYEBALL

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Thu Mar 28, 2013 7:05 pm The UN should enforce a ban on the sale of clay to Reichu.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Mar 28, 2013 8:49 pm Elect G-Max wrote: The UN should enforce a ban on the sale of clay to Reichu.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Mar 29, 2013 10:38 am Squigsquasher wrote: Correct me if I'm wrong, but I think that that's supposed to be a sensor dome rather than an eye.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Mar 29, 2013 11:25 am thewayneiac wrote: We discussed that a little in the commentary; NemZ has proposed that it is a skylight.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Fri Mar 29, 2013 1:26 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: No, a skylight wouldn't make any sense. What would it light, the stark whiteness of the entry plug? It's all the war over in the Eva's chest, so I don't see that working. There's nothing else practical for it to light either. Squig's idea of a sensor dome makes a lot of sense, but we don't see it on any other unit. Maybe it's aesthetic.

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Fri Mar 29, 2013 3:34 pm I have a crazy-ass theory. Sadamoto put it there because he thought it made Zerogouki look cooler. MIND BLOW

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 02, 2013 11:12 am Elect G-Max wrote: Sadamoto put it there because he thought it made Zerogouki look cooler.

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Tue Apr 02, 2013 7:34 pm Reichu wrote: Eva-00's biological readout and an exposed prototype skull support the binocular interpretation.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Apr 02, 2013 9:58 pm Actually I find myself having to agree (and disagree) with Elect G here. If you look at the rejected Eva-00 prototypes, it's clear that they have a quite variable number and placement of eyes. [wkimg]File:Eoe eva graveyard.jpg[/wkimg] After all these, the second eye being on the top of Eva-00's head isn't all that far fetched. (By the way, also of note is the ever present plastic coverings over the rejected Unit's heads. This covering is even present on the prototype seen in #21 (which has too many eyes in fact)) And of course, Eva-02 has the famous 4-eye set, which was confirmed in EoE. [wkimg width=300]File:M26 C049 col-disc.jpg[/wkimg] Going back to points raised in a recent discussion thread , the variability of the prototypes could be seen as a visual indicator of how lose Nerv's control over the process of Eva creation really is.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 08, 2013 2:18 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Actually I find myself having to agree (and disagree) with Elect G here. If you look at the rejected Eva-00 prototypes, it's clear that they have a quite variable number and placement of eyes.

[]:             Quote Post by Gus Hanson » Sun Apr 21, 2013 6:00 pm All this time, I've assumed it was just a one eyed bald man.
